content:
- Read the Ralph progress log on startup and inspected `.ralph/current_tasks.md` to choose the next task.
- Applied the mandatory selection rules: all non-passing bug tasks are currently blocked (`ha-action-deduping-suppresses-retry`, `ha-decide-mutation-heavy-control-flow-needs-pure-refactor`, `restore-terminal-phases-keep-ha-fencing`, `unused-backup-recovery-mode-doc-configuration`), so they are not selectable yet.
- Selected `.ralph/tasks/story-ha-functional-rewrite/05-task-rebuild-ha-tests-around-invariants-and-continuous-observers.md` as the next task because it is the next unfinished unblocked step in the HA functional rewrite chain and it directly advances the story blocking multiple deferred HA bugs; wrote that path to `.ralph/current_task.txt`.
- If I pick this up again, continue with the HA functional rewrite story and keep story task 06 next, since that is the blocker for two deferred HA bugs and part of the same migration chain.

- Read `.ralph/current_task.txt` and loaded `.ralph/tasks/story-ha-functional-rewrite/05-task-rebuild-ha-tests-around-invariants-and-continuous-observers.md`.
- Inspected `src/ha/decide.rs`, `src/ha/worker.rs`, `src/ha/e2e_partition_chaos.rs`, `src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs`, and source docs under `docs/src/` to ground the task plan in the actual codebase. Observed that `decide.rs` already has a transition matrix plus a few mutation-heavy fixtures, `worker.rs` has a large mixed test module with mutable context setup, and both e2e files already contain partial polling/finalization logic that should be extracted into a shared continuous observer layer.
- Wrote a detailed plan into the active task file, changed task status to `planning`, and ended the file with `TO BE VERIFIED` exactly as requested. The plan explicitly calls for immutable builders for pure HA tests, deeper determinism/fail-safe/contradiction coverage, a shared observer module for e2e/integration sampling, scenario-driver/observer/assertion separation, docs updates in `docs/src/contributors/testing-system.md` and `docs/src/contributors/ha-pipeline.md`, and the full final verification sequence.
- Important note for the next pass: the task description still says to defer `make test-long`, but the user-level instructions for this run require all of `make check`, `make test`, `make test-long`, and `make lint` before final completion. Preserve that stricter verification requirement when the task reaches execution/completion.
- Next immediate step after resuming: read the task file again, perform a skeptical plan review, alter at least one concrete item in the plan, replace `TO BE VERIFIED` with `NOW EXECUTE` only once the revised plan is stronger, and then stop again unless the workflow explicitly moves into execution.

- Resumed the active HA test rewrite task at the `TO BE VERIFIED` checkpoint and re-read the task markdown plus current HA module/test layout.
- Performed a skeptical plan review and changed the plan in two concrete ways: removed the stale `make test-long` deferral so the task now requires the full gate set, and pinned the shared continuous observer to a `#[cfg(test)]` HA helper module wired via `src/ha/mod.rs` instead of an unspecified shared location.
- Replaced the task marker with `NOW EXECUTE`. Next pass should not do more exploration; it should execute the revised plan, tick boxes as work lands, run `make check`, `make test`, `make test-long`, and `make lint`, then only mark passing true after all four succeed.

- Added a shared test-only HA observer module at src/ha/test_observer.rs and wired it through src/ha/mod.rs.
- Replaced inline split-brain window bookkeeping/finalization in src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs and src/ha/e2e_partition_chaos.rs with HaInvariantObserver + shared stats/assertion helpers.
- Kept timeline/artifact flow intact and moved observer-focused unit coverage into the shared module.
- Ran cargo fmt --all successfully.
- Tried cargo test ha::test_observer::unit_tests -- --nocapture, but the repo is currently blocked by unrelated compile errors in src/ha/decide.rs (world(...) arity mismatch around lines 630/758 and Some("node-b") type mismatch around line 761), so focused verification for this lane could not complete.
- Next step after context handoff: once decide.rs compiles again, rerun focused tests for ha::test_observer and touched HA e2e unit targets, then fold this lane into the main branch.
